speaker: A
lines: {A} charges in again. {B} sighs.
{A} goes one round too many. {B} looks away.
reply: We've talked about this before, {A}.
That wasn't necessary, {A}. Not this time either.

speaker: A
lines: {A} is already recounting the shares, out loud. {B} frowns.
{A} mentions a "little extra" nobody had brought up. {B} doesn't like it.
reply: We agreed to split evenly, {A}.
That's not what we agreed to, {A}.

speaker: A
lines: {A} already has fists clenched. {B} takes a step back.
{A} raises their voice. {B} is already scanning for the exit.
reply: Calm down, {A}, we're all going to pay for this.
Can we maybe avoid that, {A}?

speaker: A
lines: {A} wants to end it fast and hard. {B} raises a hand: not like this.
{A} ignores the usual restraint. {B} doesn't like the method.
reply: There's a right way to do things, {A}.
We don't hit an opponent who's down, {A}.

speaker: A
lines: {A} checks the emergency exit three times. {B} shrugs, amused.
{A} jumps at the slightest noise. {B} clearly finds it funny.
reply: Relax, {A}, it always works out for me.
Don't worry, {A}, I've never had a problem with that.

speaker: A
lines: {A} is already recalculating their cut after {B} closed the deal.
{A} whispers that they could have gotten more out of {B}, once the deal was signed.
reply: We got a good price, {A}. Be happy with that.
That was already a good deal, {A}.

speaker: A
lines: {A} calls out {B} on a slightly embellished detail of their story.
{A} points out, a bit too loudly, that it's not exactly what happened.
reply: It's a more... sellable version, {A}.
Let's just say I smoothed out the edges, {A}.

speaker: A
lines: {A} already wants to answer with force. {B} puts a firm hand on their shoulder.
{A} clenches their fists before the discussion is even over. {B} shakes their head.
reply: Not like that, {A}. We have rules.
Breathe, {A}. That's not how we do things.

speaker: A
lines: {A} refuses to deliver the finishing blow. {B} insists: honor demands it.
{A} and {B} disagree on what "doing things right" really means.
reply: Honor sometimes demands finishing what you started, {A}.
This isn't cruelty, {A}, it's the code.

speaker: A
lines: {A} takes a step back. {B} takes a hit without even flinching.
{A} watches {B} get back up like nothing happened, in disbelief.
reply: I'm fine, {A}, I've seen worse.
Don't worry about me, {A}. Worry about them.

speaker: A
lines: {A} already follows {B}'s gaze toward something shiny in the corner.
{A} asks {B} to "keep an eye" on anything that might be worth something.
reply: I saw it, {A}. We'll see if we have time.
Noted, {A}. Mission comes first, though.

speaker: A
lines: {A} just made everything blow up. {B} steps in to salvage the situation.
{A} raises their voice one more time. {B} steps in with a disarming smile.
reply: Let me handle this, {A}.
I'll fix what you just broke, {A}.

speaker: A
lines: {A} is already leaning toward the fallen opponent's belongings. {B} steps in the way.
{A} wants to search the defeated opponent's pockets. {B} finds it disgraceful.
reply: We don't loot a defeated opponent, {A}.
That's not how we do things, {A}.

speaker: A
lines: {A} is already looking for a way out. {B} stands firm, unmoving.
{A} suggests retreating. {B} refuses to turn their back this soon.
reply: We don't run like that, {A}.
There's a right way to withdraw, {A}, and this isn't it.

speaker: A
lines: {A} throws themselves into the fray again. {B} comes out unscathed, as always.
{A} has lost count of the risks taken. {B} shrugs, amused by their own luck.
reply: Another time that could have gone badly, {A}. Another time it didn't.
Don't worry, {A}, I've got good karma right now.

speaker: A
lines: {A} already wants to get back into it. {B} just takes the hits and waits.
{A} is fuming, ready to explode. {B} stays stoic, like nothing happened.
reply: Why are you getting worked up, {A}? It barely does anything to me.
Save your energy, {A}. I'll take the hits, you save yourself.

speaker: A
lines: {A} quietly steps back while {B} speaks up to defuse the situation.
{A} lets {B} negotiate, relieved not to have to lift a finger.
reply: Don't worry, {A}, I've got this. We don't need it to come to that.
Let me talk, {A}. Nobody needs to fight.
